message:
feat(conformance): add RNG statistical distribution test suite with KS tests

Introduces a new rng_statistical conformance suite that validates the
statistical properties of all supported random distributions. Each test case
draws a configurable number of samples (default 8192) and checks:

- Sample mean against expected mean within absolute tolerance
- Sample variance against expected variance within absolute tolerance
- Support bound enforcement (min/max, inclusive/exclusive)
- Kolmogorov-Smirnov goodness-of-fit against reference CDFs

The KS test infrastructure includes CDF implementations for uniform, normal,
exponential, Cauchy, logistic, Laplace, Gumbel, Rayleigh, Pareto, and power
distributions, with critical value lookup using standard KS tables.

Covers 35+ distributions: random, uniform, standard_normal, normal,
exponential, gamma, beta, geometric, lognormal, chisquare, Cauchy,
triangular, Laplace, Gumbel, Weibull, negative_binomial, F, Student-t,
noncentral chi-square, noncentral F, power, von Mises, Rayleigh, Pareto,
logistic, hypergeometric, Zipf, Wald, logseries, Maxwell, halfnormal,
truncated_normal, Lomax, and Levy.

Includes rng_statistical_cases.json fixture file, criterion benchmarks for
core ops, and updated Cargo.lock with benchmark dependencies.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
